seoTitle: Extract a Working Trial Balance in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to generate a working trial balance report in QuickBooks Online to check accuracy and prepare your financial statements.
intro: A working trial balance helps confirm that your books are accurate before you finalize financial statements. This guide walks through navigating QuickBooks Online, applying the right filters, and extracting the trial balance report you need.

steps.heading: Navigate to the reports area
steps.body: Open the Reports section in QuickBooks Online and locate the Trial Balance report from the list of available financial reports.

steps.heading: Set your filters
steps.body: Choose the date range and any account filters relevant to the period you're reviewing.

steps.heading: Extract the trial balance
steps.body: Run the report to view account balances side by side, which you can then export or print for further review.

steps.heading: Check for discrepancies
steps.body: Review the balances to identify any accounts that don't line up as expected, and investigate before finalizing your books.

faq.question: What is a working trial balance used for?
faq.answer: It's used to verify that debits and credits balance and to catch discrepancies before preparing final financial statements.

faq.question: Who typically needs this report?
faq.answer: Accountants, financial analysts, and business owners preparing for audits or closing periods commonly rely on the trial balance.

faq.question: Can this report be automated?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online allows you to save report customizations and, with some integrations, schedule recurring generation of the trial balance.

faq.question: How is a trial balance different from a Profit & Loss report?
faq.answer: A trial balance lists all account balances to check accuracy, while a Profit & Loss report summarizes revenue and expenses to show profitability.
